page: Amazon Web Services c5d.18xlarge is a compute-optimized server powered by the AWS Nitro hypervisor. It features an Intel Xeon 8124M processor with an x86_64 architecture, offering 72 virtual CPUs across 36 dedicated physical cores with hyper-threading. The system operates at a clock speed of 3.4 GHz and includes 144.0 GB of DDR4 memory running at 3 GHz, translating to 2.0 GB of RAM per core. For local storage, the instance is equipped with 1800 GB of NVMe SSD instance store volumes. Network connectivity is supported by a 25 Gbps baseline bandwidth.

Performance benchmarks indicate strong multi-core CPU capabilities, with stress-ng multi-core and Passmark CPU scores ranking in the top 25 percent. Memory bandwidth is also a notable strength, particularly for read-only cached and uncached operations, which both place in the top 25 percent. Database operations perform strongly, supported by the high-speed local NVMe storage. However, single-core CPU performance, static web serving, and Redis operations fall into the average tier, while large language model text generation speeds are weak, placing in the bottom 25 percent.

From a cost-efficiency perspective, the c5d.18xlarge provides high compute density and fast local storage without the overhead of a GPU. The inclusion of dedicated physical cores and high-speed local NVMe SSDs makes it highly suitable for compute-intensive workloads that require rapid temporary storage, such as high-performance web serving, batch processing, distributed analytics, and database operations.
description: Amazon Web Services c5d.18xlarge is a compute-optimized server utilizing the AWS Nitro hypervisor. It is equipped with an Intel Xeon 8124M x86_64 processor featuring 72 vCPUs, 36 dedicated physical cores, and a 3.4 GHz clock speed. The hardware configuration includes 144.0 GB of DDR4 memory and 1800 GB of local NVMe SSD storage, alongside a 25 Gbps baseline network bandwidth. Benchmark data shows strong multi-core CPU performance and memory read bandwidth, placing in the top 25 percent, while single-core metrics and Redis operations remain average. Due to its high compute density and fast local storage, this instance is well-suited for high-performance database operations, batch processing, and compute-heavy workloads.

tagline: A high-density compute server featuring seventy-two virtual cores, fast local NVMe storage, and strong multi-core processing capabilities.
